location: Lewis and Clark Caverns State Park, MT
title: Claustrophobia Alert
summary:
Our first stop after leaving Missoula was Lewis and Clark Caverns. Despite having lived in Missoula for about 11 years, neither Colin nor I had ever been. We arrived at the campground to discover that all of the tours for that day were full but we were able to get a spot for the next day, so we decided to spend the night. We instead went on a nice little hike to the Jefferson river where we were accompanied by many Meadowlarks.

The next morning we spent like we spend many of our mornings, reading outside with coffee in hand, before our tour that afternoon. Our tour guide was named Huckleberry and had the quintessential tour guide voice, starting every story with “now friends” in his Southern drawl. The tour itself was pretty unbelieveable. We spent about 2 hours underground, meandering through tight spaces filled with stalagmites and stalagtities (we learned the difference but I already forgot). I couldn't believe that a) we were even allowed in that cave so with many fragile areas and bats flying around and b) it only cost $15 to do so. Although I spent much of the tour slightly unsettled at being underground, we would highly recommend visiting if you are in the area!

-Helen

photos.caption: A hike we went on during our stay at Lewis and Clark Caverns.
